{{Quote|Nak Pitareeze How many times have I told you to stop teasing Artoo and Threepio?|Baron Pitareeze|<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures">[[Star Wars: Droids (1994)|''Star Wars: Droids'' (1994)]]</ref>}}
'''Nak Pitareeze''' was a young [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]], the son of [[Jarth Pitareeze|Jarth]] and [[Meg Pitareeze]]. He at one time owned [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], until they were stolen by the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Huba]]. Though the two [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were returned to Nak, they soon ventured off to [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].

During his ownership of Artoo and Threepio, Nak would at times vent his frustrations over his parents' distance by playing pranks on them, but later realized the foolishness of it all and apologized to the droids.

==Biography==
===The Kalarba Adventures===
Nak Pitareeze first encountered C-3PO and R2-D2 after they landed an escape pod into a lake next to where his family was having a picnic. His parents took R2-D2 and C-3PO into their service and they became Nak's guardians.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Shortly after the droids became Nak's guardians, he tied C-3PO to a tree and shocked R2-D2 with a restraining bolt. His grandfather, the [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Pitareeze]], stopped him and disciplined him for his actions.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Later, while the Baron Pitareeze was setting up for dinner, the pirate [[Jace Forno]] arrived and mentioned to Nak that his family could have been rich. Nak pestered his grandfather to tell him what Forno meant.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

The Baron then explained that he once worked on a cruiser known as the Pitareeze Cruiser. The plans for the Pitareeze Cruiser were stolen by the criminal [[Olag Greck]].<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Olag Greck manufactured a weak prototype that was a disaster. Afterward, the Baron Pitareeze refused to help Olag Greck fix the cruisers. This decision by the Baron Pitareeze caused Olag Greck to blame the failure of the Greck Cruiser on the Baron Pitareeze.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

After learning this, Nak attempted to sell the Baron Pitareeze latest creation, MT-5 hyperdrive unit, to Olag Greck at Olag's base on [[Hosk]]. Nak stole what he believed was an MT-5 hyperdrive unit out of his Grandfather's office and attempted to sell it to Olag Greck. C-3PO attempted to persuade Nak against this and Nak promptly switched C-3PO off. Olag discovered that the object was a hydrocompressor and destroyed it. He then held Nak hostage in exchange for one of the Baron Pitareeze hyperdrive units.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

While Nak was tied up in Olag's office, he switched 3-PO on and ordered the droid to untie him. They then stole one of Olag's ships and crash-landed on Kalarba. Nak then apologized to Threepio for playing so many tricks on him.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

When Nak and Threepio returned to the Baron's factory, they discovered that the Baron had gone to Hosk to rescue Nak. Nak and Threepio teamed up with the Baron's employee, [[Balto]], and flew to Hosk station.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

At Hosk station they successfully rescued the Baron and saved Olag's life by ending a transmission between him and an unidentified force user who was using [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|Force choke]] on Olag. Sadly, Olag had already taken the hyperdrive from the Baron.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

The Baron Pitareeze, Nak Pitareeze, C-3PO, R2-D2, and Balto made good their escape. Olag later discovered that what he believed to be a Hyperdrive unit was actually a food hydrator.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Nak's parents disciplined him for his reckless behavior. Afterward, Nak removed the restraining bolts from Threepio and R2-D2 to see if they were real friends.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Later, Nak was captured by a group of pirates, led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Huba]]. Nak attempted to escape from the pirates, but was quickly shot down and captured by Olag Grek. Olag used Nak to scout out the pirates' base. Nak soon discovered that the pirates had elected Artoo as their captain. Olag attempted to kill the pirates but was stopped by a member of the pirates known as [[Ripter]].<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

[[File:Nak_Pitareeze.png|thumb|left|120px|Nak Pitareeze.]]
Nak learned that the pirates were actually chefs who shared Olag as a mutual enemy after Olag attempted to pin an attempted murder on them. Nak told the chefs of a cave filled with Olag's treasure, and the pirates released Artoo, Threepio, and Nak in exchange for the cave's location.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

While Nak was waiting for Threepio on Hosk station, Nak mistook the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[C-3PX]] for Threepio. Threepio eventually arrived, and Nak discovered that Threepio now had an X mark on his head similar to the one that C-3PX wore. C-3PO revealed to Nak that R2-D2 had disappeared, and they went to look for him. While looking for Artoo, C-3PO was captured by Olag and sent to fight in his droid arena.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Recruiting C-3PX to help him confront Olag, Nak arrived at the arena to save Threepio. Olag managed to convince C-3PX to replace C-3PO in the droid arena. R2-D2 destroyed C-3PX during his first fight. R2-D2 returned to Nak and they were saved as the Baron Pitareeze arrived with Hosk Security.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

Back on Kalarba, Nak learned that Jace Forno was interested in hiring C-3PO and R2-D2 for a trip to [[Indobok]]. Nak agreed, and the droids left with Forno. After Forno's credit voucher bounced, Nak and his family set off to recover the droids. Upon arrival, he discovered that Artoo had been cast adrift by Forno, and Threepio had been abandoned on the planet's surface.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

The [[Pitareeze]] family set off to find Threepio, and discovered that he was telling stories to the native inhabitants, known as the [[B'rknaa]]. After an Ash storm approached, the Pitareeze family stayed the night in a cave on Indobok. The Pitareeze family then learned that Forno was returning with a band of pirates to steal the crystals that powered the B'rknaa. After the Pitareeze helped to defeat the pirates, C-3PO discovered that Indobok was an adult B'rknaa.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" />

After his adventure on Indobok, Nak attempted to buy an old droid from [[Vuldo]], a local junk dealer. After R2-D2 disappeared during the night, Nak used the old droid to look for Artoo. Upon discovery of Artoo's location, Nak accidentally knocked Vuldo unconscious. Nak learned that Vuldo had forced the town's babysitters, Q-E and 2-E, to manufacture blasters.<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Kalarba Adventures" /> In the process, Vuldo destroyed the body of their companion, U-E; after Vuldo was defeated and arrested, U-E's central processor and memory banks were installed in Nak's old droid, effectively restoring him in a new body.

==Personality and traits==
Nak was very compulsive and usually acted on impulse instead of thinking his actions through, as seen with his sudden decision to escape on a speeder bike. He was also very mischievous and did not follow directions well, which can be seen in his decision to sell his grandfather's hyperdrive unit.
